title: Improve dns-search validation, empty domains now return an error

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM but out of curiosity, does `--dns-search=.` (dot) work for anyone? It's documented in `podman-run(1)` but this is what I get, root or rootless:
```
$ podman run --rm --dns-search=. alpine_labels cat /etc/resolv.conf
Error: . is not a valid domain
```
Ok so "." means that their should not be any searches in the resolv.conf.

```
# docker run fedora cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Generated by NetworkManager
search bos.redhat.com redhat.com
nameserver 10.11.5.19
nameserver 10.5.30.160
nameserver 10.192.20.245
# NOTE: the libc resolver may not support more than 3 nameservers.
# The nameservers listed below may not be recognized.
nameserver 10.192.206.245
nameserver 10.200.0.246
```
Versus
```
# docker run --dns-search=. fedora cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 10.11.5.19
nameserver 10.5.30.160
nameserver 10.192.20.245
nameserver 10.192.206.245
nameserver 10.200.0.246
```

OK so maybe that was copied blindly from the docker man page but without actually coding it? 

My reason for testing this is that #3426, the issue this PR proposes to address, is actually a little bit of a confusion. The report quotes the man page (with dot), but then proceeds to report an error with '' (empty string). I have no idea what's going on here.
@edsantiago man page was correct, code was mostly correct except for a little overzealous in our domain checking. Opened a PR to fix.
